gitCommit.subject: WEBDEV-1475: re-enable screenshots + bounded videos so reports show evidence
gitCommit.body:
WEBDEV-1475: re-enable screenshots + bounded videos so reports show evidence

Previous change set screenshot to only-on-failure, which meant passing
runs produced a 195KB empty-looking report — no visible evidence of
what was tested. Reviewers couldn't tell if the tests actually ran.

Tuned the artifact mix:
  screenshot: 'on'
    — every test keeps screenshots. ~50-200KB each; hundreds still fit
      under the size budget.
  video: { mode: 'retain-on-failure', size: 640x360 }
    — only failed tests get videos, and at half the previous resolution.
      A failed test's webm drops from ~10MB to ~2-3MB.
  trace: 'retain-on-failure'
    — only failed tests get traces. Unchanged.

nav-pages-sanity still overrides to trace+video=off because its one-
test-many-pages design produces 150MB+ traces. Per-page screenshots
under screenshots/nav-sanity/ remain the visible evidence for that
spec.

Net: a green run now produces a report with full screenshots (~5-15MB
total), making it useful to scroll through. A red run adds smaller
videos and traces. Either way stays under the 100MB-per-file limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

errors.message:
Cannot use({ trace }) in a describe group, because it forces a new worker.
Make it top-level in the test file or put in the configuration file.
errors.snippet:
at nav-pages-sanity.spec.ts:106

[0m [90m 104 |[39m   [90m// bounded by page count) — that's the visible evidence of what was tested.[39m
 [90m 105 |[39m   [90m// Per-page videos would multiply the same way as traces, so off too.[39m
[31m[1m>[22m[39m[90m 106 |[39m   test[33m.[39muse({ trace[33m:[39m [32m'off'[39m[33m,[39m video[33m:[39m [32m'off'[39m })[33m;[39m
 [90m     |[39m        [31m[1m^[22m[39m
 [90m 107 |[39m
 [90m 108 |[39m   [36mlet[39m navPaths[33m:[39m string[] [33m=[39m [][33m;[39m
 [90m 109 |[39m[0m
